notes: First-contact visibility check — and for opencode the confrontation this scenario exists to force lands HALF-RED, recorded honestly. MODEL half: visible — the live surface is the SQLite MetricsProvider (message.data.model.modelID), and the recorded proof is the transcript's per-part _model field (= gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored, the config default); per the opencode convention (see 5-3) the staged-replay golden's summary.model_name is NULL because opencode metrics ride the provider, not the transcript replay. CONTEXT half: NOT visible with this rig — the local LM Studio slug has no LiteLLM capacity entry and opencode supplies no context-window override, so ComputeContextUtilization yields window 0 / utilization 0 / context_window_unknown=true. This is a true product gap for local-model opencode sessions, not a recording defect; unblock = a capacity-known provider model (thread via the send step's model field) or a capacity alias. The phase DSL asserts the state arc; the turn_end text asserts the model half only. KNOWN_FAILING (settle-after-exit cluster): the turn_end phase finds no working→ready transition — opencode settles only after the headless run process exits and the daemon notices, which lands outside the recording window even with a 20s post-turn sleep (two record attempts, #599). Same root cause as the documented opencode settle cluster; the arc ready→working is captured, the settle is not. Re-record without the flag when the settle behavior changes.

text: Turn completes back to ready; the model is identified (transcript _model = gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored; live surface via the SQLite MetricsProvider) — context utilization stays 0 with the local-model rig (no capacity entry), the documented opencode gap
